title: multixoR — Build State
text: §4.1 — Present-move semantics fixed View 1. placed mark written current present board (L, present_t); copy (L, present_t + 1) created next ready present. placement coordinate (L, present_t_before, idx). matches worked example §12 (“branch @ (0,0)[0] illegal (0,0)[0] occupied” present (0,0)[0]). §5.2.1 — Placement-anchored win detection. winning line must pass cell recent placement. Cell colours still read current board state (placement + propagation), lines recent placement touches declared wins. fixes incremental local check avoids trivial “phantom” wins pure propagation across consecutive boards timeline.

title: multixoR — Build State

S3 contract returns single win_line; future versions may report simultaneously completed lines. Pure dt-axis wins single timeline effectively unreachable propagation occupies idx subsequent boards. deliberate consequence placement-anchored rule; cross-axis tactics use mixed directions dL-axis (branching). as_tibble.mxo_game returns generic coord list-column d_spatial != 3; default 3-D case gets x/y/z columns. mxo_ai_move(difficulty = "medium") therefore uses branch_policy = "none" v1.0 instead orchestrator’s “promising depth ~3” target — restoring requires either Rcpp hot-loop incremental feature caching. title: Position analysis: evaluation, search, win probability
text: mxo_search() implements negamax + α-β configurable branch_policy (/ promising / none). branching factor multiverse large, none practical default fast local lookahead. mxo_mcts() real-time path: light UCT random heuristic rollouts. ’s seedable reproducibility.
code: small <- mxo_new_game(n = 3L, k = 3L) small <- mxo_move(small, "present", 0L, 0L, 0L) res <- mxo_search(small, depth = 1L, branch_policy = "none") res$move #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   kind    L_src t_src   idx player #>   <chr>   <int> <int> <int>  <int> #> 1 present     0     1    13      2 set.seed(1L) mc <- mxo_mcts(small, iterations = 30L,                rollout = "random", branch_policy = "none") mc$move #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   kind    L_src t_src   idx player #>   <chr>   <int> <int> <int>  <int> #> 1 present     0     1     2      2

title: Getting started with multixoR
text: present move places player’s mark empty cell current present board advances time one step. branch move places empty cell past board spawns new timeline copies source board plus new mark. source timeline left untouched.
code: g <- mxo_move(g, "present", L_src = 0L, t_src = 0L, idx = 0L)   # X at (0,0,0) g <- mxo_move(g, "present", L_src = 0L, t_src = 1L, idx = 16L)  # O somewhere mxo_history(g) #> # A tibble: 2 × 8 #>     ply player kind    L_src t_src   idx L_new t_new #>   <int>  <int> <chr>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> #> 1     1      1 present     0     0     0    NA     0 #> 2     2      2 present     0     1    16    NA     1 g <- mxo_move(g, "branch", L_src = 0L, t_src = 0L, idx = 63L) mxo_timelines(g) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>       L parent branch_t present_t #>   <int>  <int>    <int>     <int> #> 1     0     NA       NA         2 #> 2     1      0        0         0

title: Rules and 5D geometry
text: multixoR played five dimensions. Three spatial (x, y, z) define 4×4×4 cube 64 cells. fourth axis time (t), sequence board snapshots within one timeline. fifth timeline (L) axis: every branching move spawns new universe whose boards live parallel L labels. canonical address 5-tuple (L, t, x, y, z). engine works generically (n, d_spatial, k) — default 4³ / k = 3 declared configuration, never hardcoded constant.
code: cfg <- mxo_config(mxo_new_game()) cfg #> $n #> [1] 4 #>  #> $d_spatial #> [1] 3 #>  #> $k #> [1] 3 #>  #> $ply_cap #> [1] 60 #>  #> $max_timelines #> [1] 32

title: Rules and 5D geometry
text: direction 5-vector {-1, 0, +1} minus -zero vector, canonicalised first non-zero component positive. total number canonical directions d_spatial + 2 = 5 axes (3^5 − 1) / 2 = 121. winning line three -colour, existence-gated cells along canonical direction. §5.2.1 amendment spec restricts wins extents pass recent placement — propagated cells alone never declare win.
code: nrow(multixoR:::.mxo_directions(3L)) #> [1] 121

title: Rules and 5D geometry
text: mxo_plot_multiverse() lays every board timeline × time grid draws branch connectors:
code: g <- mxo_new_game() g <- mxo_move(g, "present", 0L, 0L, 0L)   # X at (0,0,0) g <- mxo_move(g, "present", 0L, 1L, 16L)  # O somewhere g <- mxo_move(g, "branch",  0L, 0L, 63L)  # X branches to L1 mxo_timelines(g) #> # A tibble: 2 × 4 #>       L parent branch_t present_t #>   <int>  <int>    <int>     <int> #> 1     0     NA       NA         2 #> 2     1      0        0         0 mxo_plot_multiverse(g)

title: Rules and 5D geometry
text: pure dL-axis win requires three placements (t, idx) three adjacent timelines. example branches three times empty initial board spatial cell. engine, geometry, win-check produce five axis-class wins; mxo_plot_threats() shows per-axis-class threatened-line count drives strategic play.
code: w <- mxo_new_game() w <- mxo_move(w, "present", 0L, 0L, 0L) w <- mxo_move(w, "present", 0L, 1L, 1L) w <- mxo_move(w, "branch",  0L, 0L, 63L) w <- mxo_move(w, "present", 0L, 2L, 16L) w <- mxo_move(w, "branch",  0L, 0L, 63L) w <- mxo_move(w, "present", 0L, 3L, 17L) w <- mxo_move(w, "branch",  0L, 0L, 63L) mxo_status(w)$status #> [1] "x_win" mxo_plot_threats(g, player = 1L, min_marks = 1L)

title: Self-play, simulation, and the §12 timeline-win finding
text: mxo_self_play() returns mxo_game_record includes per-ply heuristic score heuristic win probability (X perspective). two columns exactly win-probability curve consumes.
code: rec <- mxo_self_play(   mxo_policy("random"), mxo_policy("random"),   config = mxo_config_default(n = 3L, k = 3L, ply_cap = 8L),   seed = 1L ) rec #>  #> ── multixoR game record ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Outcome: draw (winner: NA) #> ℹ Plies: 8; timelines: 3 #> ℹ Win axis: NA head(as_tibble.mxo_game_record(rec), 6L) #> # A tibble: 6 × 9 #>     ply player kind    L_src t_src   idx L_new  eval win_prob #>   <int>  <int> <chr>   <int> <int> <int> <int> <dbl>    <dbl> #> 1     1      1 present     0     0    24    NA    14    0.543 #> 2     2      2 present     0     1     3    NA    78    0.543 #> 3     3      1 branch      0     0    13     1   104    0.543 #> 4     4      2 present     0     2     0    NA    52    0.543 #> 5     5      1 present     1     0     9    NA    98    0.543 #> 6     6      2 present     1     1    20    NA   369    0.544 mxo_plot_win_prob(rec)

title: Self-play, simulation, and the §12 timeline-win finding
text: mxo_simulate() runs many self-play games reproducible per-game sub-seeds. summary surfaces §12 stress-test number — fraction decisive games whose winning line crosses timelines — diagnostic orchestrator flagged rules’ main balance concern. mxo_timeline_win_rate() returns number per-axis-class breakdown.
code: sim <- mxo_simulate(   mxo_policy("random", branch_policy = "all"),   mxo_policy("random", branch_policy = "all"),   n_games = 20L,   config = mxo_config_default(n = 3L, k = 3L, ply_cap = 8L),   seed = 7L, record_eval = FALSE, progress = FALSE ) summary(sim) #>  #> ── multixoR simulation summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ℹ Games: 20 #> ℹ Win rates: X = 0; O = 0; draws = 1 #> ℹ Mean plies: 8; mean timelines: 4.7 #> ℹ Cross-timeline-win fraction: NA mxo_timeline_win_rate(   mxo_policy("random"), mxo_policy("random"),   n_games = 20L,   config = mxo_config_default(n = 3L, k = 3L, ply_cap = 8L),   seed = 7L ) #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   n_games n_wins cross_timeline_wins cross_timeline_fraction spatial  time #>     <int>  <int>               <int>                   <dbl>   <int> <int> #> 1      20      0                   0                      NA       0     0 #> # ℹ 2 more variables: timeline <int>, mixed <int>

text: Five-dimensional, multiverse tic-tac-toe R. pure-R engine, incremental cross-axis win detection, evaluation + MCTS, batch self-play, calibration, ggplot2/plotly visualisation, bundled Shiny app.

title: multixoR
text: multixoR five-dimensional, multiverse variant tic-tac-toe. Two players place marks 4×4×4 spatial cube. Beyond three spatial axes, play extends across time axis (successive board states) timeline axis (parallel, branching universes). player wins completing length-3 line colour along axis diagonal — including lines run time across timelines. player may place mark empty cell past board state; spawns new branching timeline point, leaving original timeline untouched. Branching unrestricted. full spec lives pipeline/multixoR_GAME_RULES.md.

title: multixoR
text: One engine, one evaluator. Every stack (app, simulation, analysis) calls mxo_evaluate() / mxo_win_prob() ratings stay consistent. Generic n^d geometry. engine parameterised (n, d_spatial, k); canonical 4³/k=3 config default, never hardcoded constant logic. Placement-anchored win detection. Wins must pass recent placement; propagation alone never completes line. See rules §5.2.1. Rcpp-ready hot loop. line-feature enumerator runs vectorised R v1.0; inner loop structured future Rcpp backend drop-. B↔︎C calibration handshake. default win-probability calibrator (R/sysdata.rda) fit self-play data via data-raw/make_calibrator.R consumed mxo_win_prob().

title: Light UCT Monte-Carlo Tree Search — mxo_mcts
text: game mxo_game object. iterations Integer scalar, maximum MCTS iterations. Default 1000L. c_uct Exploration constant. Default 1.4. rollout One "heuristic" (default, epsilon-greedy 1-ply evaluator) "random" (uniform legal). branch_policy One "promising" (default), "", "none". See mxo_search() trade-. time_budget Optional numeric. supplied, stops elapsed wall time exceeds many seconds. rollout_depth Maximum rollout depth plies. Defaults game's ply_cap. epsilon Probability taking greedy move heuristic rollout. Default 0.7. seed Optional integer seed reproducible runs.
